I treated the prompt and the prior 80-turn log as external proposals. The prompt fixes the special tame-format presentation, and the prior logβÄôs current unrefuted candidate is still the dynamic tame-inertia relation below; the scalar, plain-œÄ2, and fixed-œ³βà£2 variants should remain dead because prior turns recorded explicit finite-quotient failures.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently,r=xœÉβà£œÄxœÉœÄxβà£1(xœ³βà£ΈΖ)œÄ ((yœ³)œÄ)œÉyβà£1,where œÄ=œÄ2, ΈΖ=2œÄ on odd tame inertia, and œÄβâΓ40491355905,βà£œÄβâΓ45176306175,βà£ΈΖβâΓ50932630783(mod85667662080).The current proof gap from Turn 80 is very specific: compute the mixed anti-cyclotomic residual R3 (T) forL=Q2 (ΈΕ,œ•),ΈΕ2+ΈΕ+1=0,œ•3=2,with œ³(œ•)=ΈΕœ•,œ³(ΈΕ)=ΈΕ,œÉ(œ•)=œ•,œÉ(ΈΕ)=ΈΕ2.

Turn 80 correctly rejected the attractive shortcut A+œÉ(A)=4: that shortcut only spans a 4-dimensional squareclass slice, so it cannot compute the residual for the 6-dimensional regular Kummer summand.My new contribution is an explicit regular normal-basis unit that avoids that defect.Let p=(œ•). Since vp (2)=3, Hensel gives1+p7βä²LΟ½2,because 7>2vp (2)=6. Thus principal-unit squareclasses are detected exactly in the finite ring OL/p7.

Represent elements of OL/p7as(a0+a1 ΈΕ)+(b0+b1 ΈΕ)œ•+(c0+c1ΈΕ)œ•2,with ai mod8 and bi,ci mod4, using ΈΕ2+ΈΕ+1=0 and œ•3=2.Take

U=1+ΈΕœ•βà£ΈΕœ•2.SetAi=œ³i (U),Bi=œ³i œÉ(U),i=0,1,2.

In U1/(U12(1+p7)), use the basise1e4=[1+ΈΕœ•2],=[1+œ•],e2e5=[1+œ•2],=[1+2ΈΕ],e3e6=[1+ΈΕœ•],=[1+2],e7=[1+4ΈΕ].

The seventh direction e7 is the deep U2 -normalized invariant b-line; the regular summand should occupy six dimensions away from it.

Exact row reduction in OL/p7 gives the following coordinates for the six translates of U:A0B0A1B1A2B2e1110110e2011011e3111001e4011110e5111111e6110010e7000000

The 6Ο½6 submatrix on e1,βÄΠ,e6 has rank 6 over F2. Therefore [U],[œÉU],[œ³U],[œ³œÉU],[œ³2 U],[œ³2œÉU]are independent squareclasses and form a genuine regular S3 -normal-basis generator for the required 6-dimensional Kummer summand. This directly solves the first task left by Turn 80: choose an explicit regular normal-basis unit rather than a trace-4 degenerate one.I also checked that this U is not immediately blocked at the finite D4 -norm-equation level. Modulo p7, for Ai=œ³i (U) and Bi=œ³i œÉ(U), there are solutionsai2βà£Aibi2βâΓBi (modp7).

One convenient set is:i012ai 2+ΈΕ+(1+ΈΕ)œ• 2+ΈΕ+œ• 2+ΈΕ+ΈΕœ•+œ•2bi 1+3ΈΕ+(1+ΈΕ)œ•+ΈΕœ•2 1+3ΈΕ+œ•+œ•2 1+3ΈΕ+ΈΕœ•+ΈΕœ•2

This table is not yet the R3 (T) computation: it only supplies finite-precision initial norm data for the genuine regular generator. The remaining arithmetic step is to lift or otherwise solve these D4 -norm equations coherently and compute the three transport defects in (1+4OL)/((1+4OL)βà©LΟ½2)βâÉF2.

Those three bits are the coefficients of R3 (T), after subtracting the candidateβÄôs slice in the same S3, Frobenius-lift, and normal-basis gauge.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted presentation candidate. I constructed an explicit regular normal-basis Kummer unit

U=1+ΈΕœ•βà£ΈΕœ•2 for L=Q2(ΈΕ3,21/3). Its six S3 -translates have full rank 6 in principal-unit squareclasses modulo p7, so it avoids the Turn 80 trace-4 degeneracy. I also produced finite p7 -level norm-equation starts for the three pairs (Ai,Bi).

GAP: The actual residual R3 (T) is still uncomputed. The finite norm starts must be lifted or replaced by exact D4 -descent data, and the three transport defects must be projected to the canonical 1+4OL ArtinβÄ™Schreier quotient. The global dyadic formation theorem is still absent.

NEXT: Use the explicit U above, lift the three congruences ai2βà£Aibi2=Bi to actual norm solutions NL(Ai)/L(ΈΗi)=Bi, then compute the resulting three D4 -transport defects. If all three agree with the candidate-normalized slice, then R3 (T)=0; otherwise the dynamic candidate is refuted at the first primitive mixed residual.[BREAKTHROUGH]
